mechanism: Pan-ABL/FLT3 inhibitor active against FLT3-ITD and TKD mutants
additional_notes: Effective against resistant FLT3 TKD mutants including F691L

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, and VEGFR tyrosine kinases
Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases
additional_notes: Anti-proliferative in subsets of AML, including FLT3-ITD contexts

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to reduce JAK/STAT signaling
additional_notes: Variable single-agent AML activity; useful benchmark in JAK/STAT–driven contexts

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to modulate epigenetic transcription
additional_notes: Potent epigenetic inhibitor with strong AML cytotoxicity; potency can shift with serum protein binding

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to promote acetylation and apoptosis
additional_notes: Similar AML activity to vorinostat; consider serum protein binding effects

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome to disrupt protein turnover and induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Strong anti-AML effects including FLT3-ITD lines

mechanism: Irreversible 20S proteasome inhibitor causing proteotoxic stress
Inhibits mTORC1 to reduce protein synthesis and cell growth
additional_notes: Cytostatic/cytotoxic activity in AML; context-dependent responses

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway
additional_notes: Inhibits MAPK-driven AML; some activity in MOLM-13; combine in panels

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase to increase oxidative stress
additional_notes: Anti-rheumatic; consistent low-µM AML activity including MOLM-13

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial protein synthesis, impairing leukemic metabolism
additional_notes: Targets leukemic stem/progenitor metabolism; AML IC50 often low µM

mechanism: Disrupts oxidative phosphorylation and inhibits Wnt/STAT3 signaling
additional_notes: AML lines often sub-µM sensitive; check solubility limitations

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase to block the mevalonate pathway
additional_notes: Mevalonate pathway dependence in AML; micromolar activity; protein binding can shift potency
APL drug; non-APL AML lines show µM-range sensitivity; include as a reference

mechanism: Antagonizes dopamine receptors; proposed selective toxicity to leukemic cells
additional_notes: Selective toxicity to leukemic vs normal HSPCs reported; AML IC50 in low–mid µM
